# Clash Royale API Agent Reference

This repository documents the public Clash Royale API for coding agents and automation workflows.

## Entry Points

- [Index](index.md): global rules, endpoint table, response shapes, pagination, errors, caching
- [Agent Guide](AGENTS.md): agent-specific usage rules
- [Endpoint Manifest](data/endpoints.json): machine-readable endpoint routing and quirks
- [Game Mode Taxonomy](data/game-modes.json): machine-readable gameplay-mode families and API signals
- [Wiki/API Crosswalk Data](data/wiki-api-crosswalk.json): machine-readable wiki/API concept mapping
- [Gameplay Modes](game-modes.md): mode context for Trophy Road, Ranked / Path of Legend, Clan Wars, events, and side
  modes
- [Wiki/API Crosswalk](wiki-api-crosswalk.md): connect wiki gameplay concepts to endpoint/model docs
- [Models](models.md): model reference router

## Endpoint Docs

- [Players](players.md)
- [Clans](clans.md)
- [Locations](locations.md)
- [Leaderboards](leaderboards.md)
- [Tournaments](tournaments.md)
- [Global Tournaments](globaltournaments.md)
- [Cards](cards.md)
- [Events](events.md)
- [Challenges](challenges.md)

## Model Docs

- [Common Models](models/common.md)
- [Player Models](models/players.md)
- [Battle Models](models/battles.md)
- [Clan Models](models/clans.md)
- [River Race Models](models/river-race.md)
- [Location And Ranking Models](models/locations-rankings.md)
- [Leaderboard Models](models/leaderboards.md)
- [Tournament Models](models/tournaments.md)
- [Cards And Events Models](models/cards-events.md)
- [Errors And Utility Models](models/errors.md)

## Core Agent Notes

- Use relative links only.
- URL-encode tag path parameters: `#ABC` becomes `%23ABC`.
- Check `paging` before treating an `items` response as paginated.
- Treat absent and null fields differently.
- Prefer observed live behavior notes over assumptions from the official Swagger UI.
- Do not treat Trophy Road as the only activity surface; use Gameplay Modes for Ranked, Clan Wars, events, and side
  modes.
- Use Wiki/API Crosswalk when a gameplay concept needs the right API surface or an API field needs game context.
